2605.08827.title: Mental Health AI Safety Claims Must Preserve Temporal Evidence
2605.08827.paper_summary: The safety of mental health AI is often judged at the wrong temporal scale. Current evaluations typically score isolated responses, endpoint outcomes, or aggregate dialogue quality, while clinically consequential failures may arise from the order and accumulation of interactions themselves, including delayed escalation, repeated reinforcement, dependency formation, failed repair, and gradual deterioration across turns. This paper argues that this mismatch is not merely a limitation of evaluation coverage but a source of invalid safety conclusions. We introduce Temporal Safety Non-Identifiability, a formal account of why safety properties that depend on sequence, timing, accumulation, or recovery cannot be certified by protocols that discard those features. From this formalization, we develop SCOPE (Safety Claims Over Preserved Evidence) as a general principle for aligning safety claims with the evidence an evaluation actually retains, and instantiate it as SCOPE-MH, a mental-health instantiation of this reporting standard. We operationalize SCOPE-MH through a proof-of-concept on the AnnoMI dataset of expert-annotated motivational interviewing conversations, which reveals mechanisms of failure that per-turn behavior scoring does not represent. We propose SCOPE-MH as a diagnostic complement to existing evaluation infrastructure and argue that evaluation preserving temporal evidence is necessary, not optional, for safety-critical mental health AI deployment.
2605.08827.paper_summary_zh: 心理健康人工智慧的安全性常常在錯誤的時間尺度上進行評估。當前的評估通常只針對孤立的反應、最終結果或綜合對話質量進行打分，而臨床上重要的失敗可能源於互動本身的順序和累積，包括延遲升級、重複強化、依賴形成、修復失敗以及在回合中的逐漸惡化。本文主張，這種不匹配不僅僅是評估範圍的局限性，而是一種無效安全結論的來源。我們引入了時間安全非可識別性，這是一個正式的說明，解釋了依賴於序列、時間、累積或恢復的安全性特徵為何無法通過忽略這些特徵的協議來認證。基於這一形式化，我們開發了SCOPE（基於保留證據的安全聲明）作為一個通用原則，用以將安全聲明與評估實際保留的證據對齊，並將其具體化為SCOPE-MH，這是一個心理健康報告標準的具體實現。我們通過對AnnoMI數據集的概念驗證來操作化SCOPE-MH，該數據集包含專家註釋的動機訪談對話，揭示了每回合行為評分未能表現的失敗機制。我們提出SCOPE-MH作為現有評估基礎設施的診斷補充，並主張保留時間證據的評估對於安全關鍵的心理健康人工智慧部署是必要的，而非可選的。
2605.08827.author: Srimonti Dutta et.al.
